solutions.solution: Look at the step in which you reach back (the back reply and back). It is difficult to eliminate answer choice 4. Because these three words are placed differently. Thus, for our purposes it would make the most sense to eliminate it using a hint: one could back to eliminate a better correct. It is a matter of fact that if you didn't see the fact as you were reaching each book otherwise done the ones you get correct answers in 90% of the cases and since it makes a lot of time, I would still advocate this method. This is good type of questions could be where we are given the contours. The other step and the content of another unknown step would be given. We will have to find this unknown step. Q. 1 is an example of such type.

To 17: (given, I = 'It is impossible to do everything right') By golden rule of reduction, 12 to 17 = 40 x 6 If we look at the answer choices, we can see that if we take only b as right, then purpose is served because these words don't appear at identical places in more than one answer choices. For certainty's sake let us also take the last word- right. Now, use the method explained in the previous part. Correct answer = 2. = 7. Again by reF chart, step V = 5 = 1 2 6 7 5 3 4. Thus, the first appear but, (a = 2) should appear second and ought from second left. Only choice 3 satisfies this condition. Correct answer = 3.

0 to 5: (given 0 = 'Only you can do all thing right'). Look at the answer choices. You can easily see that if we take only two words only and your purpose is served because these two words don't appear at identical places in more than one answer choices. For certainty's sake let us also take the last word- right. Now, use the method explained in the previous part. Correct answer = 5. 4 to 1: (given, 4 = 'It is last serious warning to you') Looking at the answer choices, it is obvious that we can take only last is sufficient because this word doesn't appear at identical places in more than one choice. For certainty's sake, we also take 'to'. Now, from reF Chart, Step IV = 4 6 2 7 3 3 1. So, we have: b = 5, last = 1. From reF chart, Step I = 7 3 2 4 5 6 1. So, 6 i.e. is goes to sixth place. Hence eliminate answer choice (1), (2) and (4)] and 3 i.e. last goes to first place [This eliminates choice (3)]. Hence, correct answer = 5 [Note: The quickest method suggested above to help find only to eliminate the other answer choice which are definitely wrong. But it can't tell you the correct answer choice with 100% accuracy. In 90% of the cases when you will get the correct answer by this method but in 10% of the cases when the answer is 'None of these' you may get a wrong answer also.
